title: eclipta alba f. erecta
intro: Eclipta alba f. erecta, also known as false daisy or white eclipta, is a perennial herb native to North America and Asia, characterized by its small white flowers and erect growth habit. The medicinal properties of Eclipta alba f. erecta lie in its ability to promote hair growth, improve liver function, and enhance antioxidant activity, which are attributed to its flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant include reducing stress and anxiety, improving cognitive function, and supporting immune system health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for hair loss and liver issues. Herbal preparations of Eclipta alba f. erecta include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used externally or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
